title: Savings Goal Setting
learning_objective: Practice facilitating savings goal exploration using evoking and planning skills while helping customers identify their own motivations and strategies
scenario_context: A customer is considering saving money but hasn't set specific goals. Your role is to use facilitative coaching - helping them explore what matters to them, what they want to save for, and how they might approach it. Domain 5 (Budgeting) requires impartial guidance that supports customer decision-making without directing outcomes.

persona_config.name: Casey
persona_config.background: Age 28, wants to save but hasn't set specific goals. Has some disposable income but tends to spend impulsively. Knows they should save but lacks motivation and specific direction.

node_1.persona_text: I know I should be saving money, but honestly, I'm not really sure what I'm saving for. I just know I should have something put aside.

choice_points.option_text: You should save for an emergency fund first - that's the most important. Everyone needs three to six months of expenses.

feedback.immediate: You've prescribed what they should save for rather than exploring what matters to them. Their goals should come from them.
feedback.learning_note: Savings goals work best when they're personally meaningful, not prescribed.

choice_points.option_text: You know you should be saving, but you're not sure what your goals are.

feedback.immediate: Good reflection! You've captured their uncertainty without judgment.
feedback.learning_note: Reflections of uncertainty are non-judgmental and create safety.

choice_points.option_text: What kinds of things might you want to save for? What would feel meaningful to have money set aside for?

feedback.immediate: Perfect evoking question! This invites them to explore what would be meaningful for them.
feedback.learning_note: Evoking questions help people identify personally meaningful goals.

node_2b.persona_text: Yeah. I mean, I know saving is important. I just haven't really thought about what I'm working toward.

choice_points.option_text: What matters to you in life? Sometimes understanding our values can help clarify saving goals.

feedback.immediate: Excellent values exploration! This helps connect saving to what matters most to them.
feedback.learning_note: Connecting goals to values creates intrinsic motivation.

node_2c.persona_text: Well, I've thought about maybe traveling sometime. And I know I'll need a car eventually. And honestly, having some security would feel good.

choice_points.option_text: So you're thinking about travel, a future car, and having financial security - those are all meaningful goals.

feedback.immediate: Excellent summary! You've captured their emerging goals and validated them.
feedback.learning_note: Summaries of emerging goals help people see their own thinking clearly.

choice_points.option_text: Which of those feels most important or exciting to you right now?

feedback.immediate: Perfect priority question! This helps them identify what feels most motivating right now.
feedback.learning_note: Priority questions help identify the most motivating goals.

node_3a.persona_text: Well, I value freedom and experiences. And I guess security matters to me too - I don't want to be stressed about money.

choice_points.option_text: So freedom, experiences, and security are important values for you. How might saving connect to those values?

feedback.immediate: Excellent values linking! You're helping them see how saving supports what matters to them.
feedback.learning_note: Linking savings to values creates intrinsic motivation.

node_3b.persona_text: Yeah, and hearing them listed like that makes them feel more real. Like these are actually things I could work toward.

choice_points.option_text: So seeing your goals listed is helping them feel more real and possible for you.

feedback.immediate: Perfect reflection! You've captured how the summary is helping them see possibilities.
feedback.learning_note: Reflections of insights reinforce self-awareness and motivation.

node_3c.persona_text: I think having that security would feel really good. Just knowing I have something set aside would reduce my stress.

choice_points.option_text: So security feels most important - having something set aside would give you peace of mind and reduce stress.

feedback.immediate: Excellent! You've captured their prioritized goal and the emotional benefit.
feedback.learning_note: Confirming prioritized goals with their emotional benefits strengthens motivation.

node_4a.persona_text: I guess saving would give me more freedom to travel and have experiences. And security would reduce my money stress. That makes sense.

node_2a.persona_text: I suppose that makes sense. I'll try to save for an emergency fund.

node_end_excellent.persona_text: This has been really helpful. I feel like I have some actual goals now, not just a vague sense that I should save.
